backlog.title: Configuration-Precedence & Diagnostics Bugfix
backlog.description: Fix the six issues found during end-to-end PRD validation. The changeset is concentrated in two areas: (1) the config-precedence layer for default-true boolean fields (auto_stage_all, multi_turn_fallback) which cannot be persistently disabled through any correctly-documented method (Major Issues 1-3), and (2) minor diagnostics gaps in the verbose/payload/model-shadowing surfaces (Minor Issues 4-6). All six PRD claims have been validated against the codebase with exact line numbers — see plan/013_b8a415cc6e79/bugfix/001_6cae455fc933/architecture/. No changes to the atomic-commit core, freeze invariants, or rescue protocol are required.

milestones.title: Restore persistent disable for default-true booleans (Issues 1-3)
milestones.description: A core documented option (auto_stage_all) cannot be persistently disabled through any correctly-documented method: the TOML path silently ignores false (Issue 1), the documented snake_case git-config key is un-settable (Issue 2), and there is no env var (Issue 3). Together these mean the ONLY working persistent escape is an undocumented camelCase git-config key — and even that hits the same overlay bug. This milestone converts AutoStageAll and MultiTurnFallback to *bool (mirroring the proven DiffContext *int pattern), adds the missing env vars, and reconciles the git-config docs.

tasks.title: Convert AutoStageAll & MultiTurnFallback to *bool precedence-aware overlay (Issue 1)
tasks.description: The core fix: both default-true booleans use an 'only-true-propagates' overlay (if src.X { dst.X = true }) that silently drops an explicit false. Convert them to *bool end-to-end (nil = inherit lower layer; non-nil incl. *false = explicit override), following the 1:1 proven DiffContext *int precedent. This is a cross-cutting but mechanical type change (~12 code sites).

subtasks.title: *bool conversion: struct fields, defaults, accessors, materialize/overlay, git layer, and all consumers
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: This is the ONLY default-true-bool bug fix. Verified codebase state in plan/013_b8a415cc6e79/bugfix/001_6cae455fc933/architecture/system_context.md and research_config_precedence.md. The proven pattern to mirror is DiffContext *int (config.go:77, accessor DiffContextValue() at config.go:169-176, materialize guard at file.go:237-243, overlay guard at file.go:375-382). Helper boolPtr already exists at config.go:7. This subtask is an ATOMIC compile-clean change: the type change touches every assignment and read site, so ALL sites must be updated together.
2. INPUT: The current types are plain bool: Config.AutoStageAll (config.go:65), Config.MultiTurnFallback (config.go:81), fileDefaults.AutoStageAll (file.go:45), fileGeneration.MultiTurnFallback (file.go:55). Defaults() sets them true (config.go:345, config.go:353). The broken merge guards are in materialize (file.go:221-222 `if d.AutoStageAll { c.AutoStageAll = true }`, file.go:247-250 for MultiTurnFallback) and overlay (file.go:343-344, file.go:386-389).
3. LOGIC: Make these exact changes (all verified line numbers):
   a. config.go: Change `AutoStageAll bool` → `AutoStageAll *bool` (keep toml tag `auto_stage_all`); change `MultiTurnFallback bool` → `MultiTurnFallback *bool` (keep toml tag `multi_turn_fallback`).
   b. config.go Defaults(): Change `AutoStageAll: true` → `AutoStageAll: boolPtr(true)`; `MultiTurnFallback: true` → `MultiTurnFallback: boolPtr(true)`.
   c. config.go: Add accessors mirroring DiffContextValue(): `func (c Config) AutoStageAllValue() bool { if c.AutoStageAll != nil { return *c.AutoStageAll }; return true }` and `func (c Config) MultiTurnFallbackValue() bool { if c.MultiTurnFallback != nil { return *c.MultiTurnFallback }; return true }`.
   d. file.go: Change fileDefaults.AutoStageAll to `*bool` and fileGeneration.MultiTurnFallback to `*bool`.
   e. file.go materialize(): Change `if d.AutoStageAll { c.AutoStageAll = true }` → `if d.AutoStageAll != nil { c.AutoStageAll = d.AutoStageAll }` (pointer copy). Same pattern for MultiTurnFallback at file.go:247-250.
   f. file.go overlay(): Change `if src.AutoStageAll { dst.AutoStageAll = true }` → `if src.AutoStageAll != nil { dst.AutoStageAll = src.AutoStageAll }`. Same for MultiTurnFallback at file.go:386-389.
   g. git.go:158-161: Change `c.AutoStageAll = v` → `c.AutoStageAll = boolPtr(v)` (git-config layer produces *bool).
   h. Adapt ALL consumer sites to use accessors: internal/cmd/default_action.go:121 (`cfg.AutoStageAll` → `cfg.AutoStageAllValue()`), default_action.go:382 (`cfg.AutoStageAll` → `cfg.AutoStageAllValue()`), internal/generate/generate.go:394 (`cfg.MultiTurnFallback` → `cfg.MultiTurnFallbackValue()`), internal/hook/exec.go:226 (`cfg.MultiTurnFallback` → `cfg.MultiTurnFallbackValue()`), pkg/stagecoach/stagecoach.go:623 (`cfg.MultiTurnFallback` → `cfg.MultiTurnFallbackValue()`).
   i. Verify `config init` template output still serializes `auto_stage_all = true` correctly (the TOML encoder handles *bool by dereferencing; if not, add a helper). Grep for any other `.AutoStageAll` or `.MultiTurnFallback` reads/writes in non-test code.
   j. MOCKING: None needed — pure internal type refactor. The stubagent provider is used for integration tests in S2.
4. OUTPUT: Compiles clean. Unit tests: accessor returns true for nil (default), *true, *false. Table-driven overlay/materialize test proving `auto_stage_all = false` in fileConfig produces a Config where AutoStageAllValue() == false through the full layer stack (global TOML → repo TOML → git config). The existing file_test.go and load_test.go bool-assertions must be updated from bool to *bool / accessor comparisons.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update docs/configuration.md line 166 (the multi_turn_fallback limitation note currently says 'you cannot disable it by setting multi_turn_fallback = false in a config file in this revision' — rewrite to state it CAN now be disabled via TOML/env/git-config, referencing the new *bool behavior). Also update docs/configuration.md line 157 (the no_verify note references 'the same only-true-propagates file pattern as auto_stage_all' — auto_stage_all no longer has that limitation; adjust the cross-reference to note auto_stage_all/multi_turn_fallback are now *bool while no_verify/push remain only-true-propagates since they are default-false). These ride WITH the work.

subtasks.title: End-to-end integration tests: TOML/git-config false disables auto-stage and multi-turn fallback
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: The PRD Issue 1 provides an exact reproduction recipe (see h3.0 'Reproduction'). The test must prove the end-to-end behavioral fix: a TOML file setting `auto_stage_all = false` causes stagecoach to exit 2 'Nothing to commit' instead of auto-staging. The `//go:build e2e` suite already covers happy-path auto-stage; add the negative case. See architecture/system_context.md §Config Precedence for the layer model.
2. INPUT: The *bool conversion from P1.M1.T1.S1 is complete. Config.AutoStageAllValue() accessor is available. The stub provider (cmd/stubagent) and the e2e test harness exist.
3. LOGIC: Write integration tests (go:build e2e or standard _test.go as appropriate):
   a. Reproduce PRD Issue 1: `git init`, seed a commit, leave a dirty working tree (un-staged `b.txt`). Create a config file with `[defaults] auto_stage_all = false` and a stub provider. Run stagecoach with that config. ASSERT: exit code 2, no commit created, working tree still dirty.
   b. Positive control: same setup but `auto_stage_all = true` (or no setting). ASSERT: exit 0, commit created, working tree clean.
   c. multi_turn_fallback: config with `multi_turn_fallback = false`. ASSERT: MultiTurnFallbackValue() == false, and the fallback path does not activate on a large diff (the generate/hook consumers respect false).
   d. Layer precedence: set `auto_stage_all = false` in global TOML, then override to true via env STAGECOACH_AUTO_STAGE_ALL=true (after P1.M1.T2.S1 lands). ASSERT: true wins (env > file). If T2 has not landed yet, use the git-config layer to test override.
   e. MOCKING: Use the stub provider (cmd/stubagent) for deterministic output. Use throwaway repos (mkdtemp + git init).
4. OUTPUT: Passing integration tests proving false propagates through TOML → resolved Config → consumer behavior, for both auto_stage_all and multi_turn_fallback.
5. DOCS: none — no user-facing/config/API surface change (tests only).

tasks.title: Add STAGECOACH_AUTO_STAGE_ALL & STAGECOACH_MULTI_TURN_FALLBACK env vars (Issue 3)
tasks.description: There is no env-var source for auto_stage_all (or multi_turn_fallback). With the TOML path broken (Issue 1, fixed in T1) and the documented git-config key invalid (Issue 2, fixed in T3), there is no working correctly-documented persistent way to disable auto_stage_all. Add the missing env vars as DIRECT-set cases in loadEnv, mirroring STAGECOACH_PUSH/STAGECOACH_NO_VERIFY.

subtasks.title: Add DIRECT-set env cases for STAGECOACH_AUTO_STAGE_ALL and STAGECOACH_MULTI_TURN_FALLBACK in loadEnv
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Verified in research_config_precedence.md §2 — loadEnv (load.go:229-323) has NO case for STAGECOACH_AUTO_STAGE_ALL or STAGECOACH_MULTI_TURN_FALLBACK. The pattern to mirror is STAGECOACH_PUSH (load.go:301) and STAGECOACH_NO_VERIFY (load.go:310): presence check via os.LookupEnv + strconv.ParseBool + wrapped error + DIRECT assignment. After P1.M1.T1.S1, the fields are *bool, so DIRECT set uses boolPtr(b) (non-nil = explicit override, so false works).
2. INPUT: Config.AutoStageAll and Config.MultiTurnFallback are now *bool (from P1.M1.T1.S1). The boolPtr helper exists at config.go:7. loadEnv is at load.go:229-323.
3. LOGIC: In loadEnv (internal/config/load.go), add two cases mirroring STAGECOACH_PUSH (load.go:301-308):
   a. `STAGECOACH_AUTO_STAGE_ALL`: `if v, ok := os.LookupEnv("STAGECOACH_AUTO_STAGE_ALL"); ok && v != "" { b, err := strconv.ParseBool(v); if err != nil { return fmt.Errorf("STAGECOACH_AUTO_STAGE_ALL: %w", err) }; cfg.AutoStageAll = boolPtr(b) }`. Place near the STAGECOACH_PUSH block (load.go:301).
   b. `STAGECOACH_MULTI_TURN_FALLBACK`: identical pattern, sets `cfg.MultiTurnFallback = boolPtr(b)`.
   c. Both are DIRECT (non-nil pointer) so a `false` value correctly overrides the default-true lower layers — this is the key difference from the old bool pattern.
   d. MOCKING: Use os.Setenv / os.Unsetenv in tests with t.Setenv (Go 1.17+). Test true, false, invalid value (error), and unset (no-op) for both vars.
4. OUTPUT: loadEnv handles both env vars. Unit tests prove: STAGECOACH_AUTO_STAGE_ALL=false → AutoStageAllValue()==false; STAGECOACH_AUTO_STAGE_ALL=true → true; STAGECOACH_AUTO_STAGE_ALL=invalid → load error; unset → no change. Same for MULTI_TURN_FALLBACK. Env overrides TOML (precedence layer 5 > layers 2-4).
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Add two rows to the env-var table in docs/configuration.md (lines ~169-199, after STAGECOACH_PUSH row at line 199): `STAGECOACH_AUTO_STAGE_ALL | --no-auto-stage (inverse) | Auto-stage all when nothing staged (true=enable, false=disable) | STAGECOACH_AUTO_STAGE_ALL=false stagecoach` and `STAGECOACH_MULTI_TURN_FALLBACK | (no flag) | Enable lossless multi-turn fallback on large diffs (true=enable, false=disable) | STAGECOACH_MULTI_TURN_FALLBACK=false stagecoach`. Note: --no-auto-stage is the per-invocation inverse; the env var is the persistent form. These ride WITH the work.

tasks.title: Reconcile git-config key documentation: snake_case → camelCase (Issue 2)
tasks.description: The documented git-config key stagecoach.auto_stage_all (snake_case) is invalid — git rejects underscores in the final config-key segment. The implementation reads camelCase stagecoach.autoStageAll. The docs are the side that needs fixing: two lines in docs/configuration.md use the wrong spelling.

subtasks.title: Fix docs/configuration.md git-config key spelling (lines 210 + 218) and verify all multi-word keys are camelCase
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Verified in research_gitconfig_keys.md. The implementation (internal/config/git.go) reads camelCase keys exclusively — all 8 multi-word keys are camelCase (autoStageAll, stripCodeFence, noVerify, maxDiffBytes, maxMdLines, tokenLimit, diffContext, maxDuplicateRetries, subjectTargetChars). git forbids underscores in the final dotted segment of a config key name, so `git config stagecoach.auto_stage_all false` fails with 'error: invalid key'. The docs have exactly TWO wrong lines.
2. INPUT: The exact discrepancies (from research_gitconfig_keys.md §3):
   - docs/configuration.md line 210 (INI example): `auto_stage_all = true` → should be `autoStageAll = true`
   - docs/configuration.md line 218 (table row): `| stagecoach.auto_stage_all | bool | git config --get --bool stagecoach.auto_stage_all | Auto-stage all when nothing staged |` → should use `stagecoach.autoStageAll` in all three occurrences in that row.
3. LOGIC:
   a. Edit docs/configuration.md line 210: change `auto_stage_all = true` to `autoStageAll = true` in the git-config INI example block.
   b. Edit docs/configuration.md line 218: change the table row to use `stagecoach.autoStageAll` for the Key column, the Reads-with column, and any inline reference.
   c. Verify (grep) that NO other git-config keys (prefixed `stagecoach.` or inside a `[stagecoach]` INI block) use snake_case. The TOML config-file keys (`[defaults] auto_stage_all`, `[generation] max_diff_bytes`, etc.) are a DIFFERENT layer and legitimately use snake_case — do NOT change those.
   d. Secondary finding (optional, out of strict PRD scope): the git-config table (lines 215-226) is missing 6 keys that git.go reads (stagecoach.verbose, stagecoach.noVerify, stagecoach.maxDiffBytes, stagecoach.maxMdLines, stagecoach.maxDuplicateRetries, stagecoach.subjectTargetChars). Consider adding these for completeness but prioritize the snake_case fix.
   e. MOCKING: None — pure documentation.
4. OUTPUT: Every git-config key in docs/configuration.md matches the spelling read by internal/config/git.go. `git config stagecoach.autoStageAll false` works and correctly disables auto-stage (cross-verified by the existing git_test.go coverage of git.go:158).
5. DOCS: [Mode A] This subtask IS the documentation fix. The two edits above are the entire deliverable. No separate docs task needed for this issue.

milestones.title: Minor diagnostics fixes & changeset-level documentation sync (Issues 4-6)
milestones.description: Three independent minor issues: (4) STAGECOACH_VERBOSE=2 is rejected with an opaque parse error instead of a clear message; (5) the --verbose payload-size line is a no-op for positional/flag-delivery providers; (6) --model is silently shadowed by per-role config with no hint. These are in different packages (config, provider, cmd) and have no interdependencies.

tasks.title: Handle STAGECOACH_VERBOSE=2 gracefully (Issue 4)
tasks.description: STAGECOACH_VERBOSE=2 (documented in PRD §19 for stdin-contents logging) fails with an opaque strconv.ParseBool error because Config.Verbose is a bool. The code comments acknowledge VERBOSE=2 as deferred. The minimal fix: gracefully reject '2' with a clear, actionable message instead of an opaque parse error. Full implementation (promoting Verbose to int) is out of scope for a bugfix.

subtasks.title: Gracefully reject STAGECOACH_VERBOSE=2 with a clear 'not yet supported' message
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Verified in research_provider_verbose.md Issue 4 section. Config.Verbose is bool (config.go:70). loadEnv parses STAGECOACH_VERBOSE with strconv.ParseBool at load.go:246-251, which rejects '2' with 'strconv.ParseBool: parsing "2": invalid syntax'. The verbose.go comments (lines 30-33, 62-64) acknowledge VERBOSE=2 as deferred/out-of-scope. PRD §19 documents stagecoach_VERBOSE=2 for stdin-contents logging.
2. INPUT: loadEnv at load.go:246-251 is the parsing site. The current error path: ParseBool fails → wraps error → Load returns error → exit 1.
3. LOGIC: In loadEnv's STAGECOACH_VERBOSE handler (load.go:246-251), BEFORE calling strconv.ParseBool, check if the value is exactly "2" (or any value > "1" that isn't a valid bool). If so, return a CLEAR error: `fmt.Errorf("STAGECOACH_VERBOSE=2 is not yet supported (stdin-contents logging is deferred); use 0/1/true/false")`. This replaces the opaque parse error with an actionable message. Alternatively, if ParseBool fails and the raw value is "2", post-process the error to append the clear explanation. Keep Config.Verbose as bool (do NOT promote to int — that is a cross-cutting feature change, not a bugfix). Update the verbose.go comments (lines 30-33, 62-64) to reflect that VERBOSE=2 now produces a clear rejection rather than being 'un-parseable'.
4. OUTPUT: STAGECOACH_VERBOSE=2 → clear error message mentioning 'not yet supported' and suggesting 0/1/true/false. STAGECOACH_VERBOSE=1/true/0/false still work unchanged. Unit test: env=2 → error contains 'not yet supported'; env=1 → true, no error; env=0 → false.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update docs/configuration.md line 179 (the STAGECOACH_VERBOSE table row) to add a note: 'VERBOSE=2 (stdin-contents logging) is not yet implemented and produces a clear error if set.' Also update docs/cli.md if it mentions VERBOSE levels. These ride WITH the work.

tasks.title: Fix --verbose payload-size line for positional/flag-delivery providers (Issue 5)
tasks.description: executor.go calls VerbosePayload(len(spec.Stdin)), but for positional/flag-delivery providers spec.Stdin is empty (payload is in spec.Args), so VerbosePayload(0) is a no-op and no payload-size line is emitted. FR50's purpose (exposing whether the token-limit gate ran) is defeated for those providers. Fix: record payload size in CmdSpec at the Render call site where the delivery mode is known.

subtasks.title: Add PayloadBytes field to CmdSpec; compute in Render delivery switch; use in executor
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Verified in research_provider_verbose.md Issue 5 section. executor.go:64 calls `vb.VerbosePayload(len(spec.Stdin))`. For stdin delivery, spec.Stdin carries the payload (size correct). For positional/flag delivery, the payload is appended to spec.Args (render.go:167-177 switch) and spec.Stdin=="", so VerbosePayload(0) hits the `bytes <= 0` guard (verbose.go:97) and emits nothing. CmdSpec (render.go:22-29) intentionally does NOT carry the delivery mode. Delivery modes: stdin (default, most providers), positional (cursor), flag (user manifests). Render has TWO delivery switches: render.go:163-178 (Render) and render.go:274-282 (RenderMultiTurn).
2. INPUT: CmdSpec struct at render.go:22-29. Render at render.go:89-185 (delivery switch at 163-178). RenderMultiTurn at render.go (delivery switch at 274-282). executor.go:64 is the single VerbosePayload call site. VerbosePayload at verbose.go:94-100.
3. LOGIC:
   a. Add a field `PayloadBytes int` to the CmdSpec struct (render.go:22-29). Document: 'size of the delivered payload in bytes (len(payload) regardless of delivery mode); 0 if no payload. Set by Render for all delivery modes so the executor can report it via VerbosePayload without knowing the delivery mode.'
   b. In Render's delivery switch (render.go:163-178), set `spec.PayloadBytes = len(payload)` for ALL three cases (stdin, positional, flag) — after constructing spec, before returning. The payload variable is already in scope.
   c. In RenderMultiTurn's delivery switch (render.go:274-282), set `spec.PayloadBytes = len(payload)` similarly.
   d. In executor.go:64, change `vb.VerbosePayload(len(spec.Stdin))` → `vb.VerbosePayload(spec.PayloadBytes)`. This works for all delivery modes because Render sets PayloadBytes uniformly.
   e. MOCKING: Unit-test Render by constructing a manifest with each delivery mode (stdin/positional/flag) and asserting CmdSpec.PayloadBytes equals len(payload). Unit-test executor with a CmdSpec where PayloadBytes is set and Verbose is on → assert the 'DEBUG: payload:' line is emitted for positional/flag (was previously absent).
4. OUTPUT: `--verbose` prints the payload-size line for ALL provider delivery modes (stdin, positional, flag). Existing stdin-provider tests still pass (PayloadBytes == len(spec.Stdin) for stdin). New tests prove positional/flag providers now emit the line.
5. DOCS: none — no user-facing/config/API surface change. This is an internal diagnostics correctness fix (the verbose output format is unchanged; it now appears for more providers). The FR50 documentation already describes the payload-size line; this makes it match.

tasks.title: Add --verbose hint for --model/--provider shadowing by per-role config (Issue 6)
tasks.description: When a global --model is set but a per-role [role.message] model exists in config, ResolveRoleModel silently picks the per-role model and the user's --model is ignored with no warning. This is correct per FR-R3 but is a UX footgun. Add a one-line --verbose hint when an explicit --model/--provider is shadowed, and document the precedence gotcha in CLI help.

subtasks.title: Detect --model/--provider shadowing and emit a VerboseWarn hint in default_action.go
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Verified in research_provider_verbose.md Issue 6 section. The precedence path: --model sets cfg.Model (global, load.go:353-355); [role.message].model sets cfg.Roles['message'].Model (file.go overlay); ResolveRoleModel (roles.go:34-57) makes per-role beat global. default_action.go:176-205 resolves the message role and runs ValidateModel on the RESOLVED model. When cfg.Roles['message'].Model is set, the global --model is silently never used. No shadow diagnostic exists anywhere (grep-confirmed). The verbose sink is available at default_action.go:399.
2. INPUT: default_action.go around lines 176-205 has `roleProvider, roleModel, _ := config.ResolveRoleModel('message', *cfg)` and the flagset `fs` is in scope (to check fs.Changed). The Verbose sink (`vb *ui.Verbose`) is available in the action. VerboseWarn(msg string) exists at verbose.go (prints 'DEBUG: <msg>' when on).
3. LOGIC: In default_action.go after the ResolveRoleModel('message') call (~line 176), add a shadow check:
   a. If `fs.Changed('model')` AND `cfg.Roles['message'].Model != ''` (or more generally, roleModel != cfg.Model): emit `vb.VerboseWarn('note: --model shadowed by [role.message].model; use --message-model to override')`.
   b. If `fs.Changed('provider')` AND `cfg.Roles['message'].Provider != ''`: emit `vb.VerboseWarn('note: --provider shadowed by [role.message].provider; use --message-provider to override')`.
   c. These are --verbose-only hints (no behavioral change, no stderr noise when not verbose). They do NOT change the precedence — FR-R3 is correct.
   d. MOCKING: Unit-test with a Config that has Roles['message'].Model set and fs.Changed('model')==true; assert the VerboseWarn is emitted to the buffer. Test that no warning is emitted when per-role model is empty.
4. OUTPUT: When --verbose is on and --model (or --provider) is shadowed by a per-role config entry, a one-line 'DEBUG: note: ...' hint is printed. No change to behavior or exit codes.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Add a precedence note to the --model help text in docs/cli.md (and root.go:168 flag description if feasible): e.g. '--model sets the global default; a [role.<role>] model in config takes precedence for that role — use --<role>-model to override'. Also cross-reference this in docs/configuration.md's role-config section. These ride WITH the work.

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation
tasks.description: Final cross-cutting documentation sweep. The per-feature doc updates (Mode A) rode with each implementing subtask; this task (Mode B) covers README.md and any overview docs that span the whole changeset and only make sense once all fixes are in place. It ensures no stale references to the old 'cannot disable auto_stage_all' behavior, the old snake_case git-config key, or the old opaque VERBOSE=2 error survive in the overview docs.

subtasks.title: Sweep README.md and overview docs for stale config-precedence references
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: README.md line 264 documents the config precedence order. After this changeset, auto_stage_all and multi_turn_fallback are fully configurable through all layers (TOML/env/git-config/flags). The old limitations (cannot disable via file, no env var, invalid git-config key, opaque VERBOSE=2 error) are all resolved.
2. INPUT: All implementing subtasks (P1.M1.T1.S1 through P1.M2.T6.S1) are complete. Their per-feature Mode A doc updates (docs/configuration.md env table, git-config keys, limitation notes, VERBOSE note, --model precedence note) are already done.
3. LOGIC:
   a. README.md: grep for 'auto_stage_all', 'autoStageAll', 'STAGECOACH_AUTO_STAGE_ALL', 'STAGECOACH_VERBOSE', 'multi_turn', 'cannot disable', 'limitation'. Update any feature blurb or capability list that references the old limitations. If README has a config/features section, ensure it notes auto_stage_all/multi_turn_fallback are configurable through all layers.
   b. docs/configuration.md: final consistency sweep — verify the TOML auto_stage_all note (line 87 template is fine), the defaults table (line 133 shows true default — fine), the git-config table (line 218 fixed in T3), the env table (new rows added in T2), the multi_turn_fallback note (line 166 fixed in T1), the no_verify note (line 157 fixed in T1), and the STAGECOACH_VERBOSE row (line 179 updated in T4). Verify no stale 'cannot disable' or 'only-true-propagates' references remain for auto_stage_all/multi_turn_fallback.
   c. docs/cli.md: verify --model help (T6), --verbose/VERBOSE documentation (T4), and any env-var listing is consistent with the new STAGECOACH_AUTO_STAGE_ALL/STAGECOACH_MULTI_TURN_FALLBACK vars.
   d. docs/how-it-works.md: grep for 'auto_stage_all' references (research noted line 327) — update if it references the old limitation.
   e. MOCKING: None — pure documentation.
4. OUTPUT: All overview/README docs are consistent with the resolved behavior. No stale 'limitation' language for the fixed fields. A reader following the docs can successfully disable auto_stage_all via TOML, env, or git-config.
5. DOCS: [Mode B] This subtask IS the changeset-level documentation sync. It depends on every implementing subtask so it runs last and catches any remaining drift.
